source_jobs.title: AR Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Accounts Receivable Specialist 

The Role: 

The Accounts Receivable Specialist plays a crucial role in our revenue cycle management team, dedicated to ensuring the accurate, compliant, and timely billing and reimbursement of multi-specialty medical professional services from diverse insurance payers and patients. This pivotal position involves meticulous attention to detail in the collection of unpaid patient accounts, setting and achieving specific goals, proficiently managing re-billing, payment posting, contractual write-offs, adjustments, and appeals in accordance with multi-state and federal insurance regulations. The ideal candidate will demonstrate a strong commitment to maintaining revenue integrity while upholding the highest standards of professionalism and compliance.



Why work for Prompt RCM?

 - BIG Challenges: Here at Prompt, we are solving complex and unique problems that have plagued the healthcare industry since the dawn of time.

 - Talented People: Prompt didn't happen by chance, it's a team of incredibly talented and proven individuals who all made their mark before joining forces to build the greatest software on the planet for rehab therapists.

 - Healthy Approach: This isn't an investment bank, we work long hours when it's needed, but at Prompt you own your workload and the entire organization takes a liking to smart work (over hard work).

 - Positive Impact: Prompt helps outpatient rehab organizations treat more patients and deliver better care with less environmental waste. That means less surgery and less narcotic-based pain treatment, all while turning a paper-heavy industry digital. We aren't enthralled with patting ourselves on the back everyday, but it does feel good :)



Key Responsibilities:

 - Prepare and accurately resubmit comprehensive corrected claims to various insurance companies, adhering closely to specific payer guidelines and contractual requirements, both electronically and via paper submission.

 - Conduct thorough analysis of first pass rejected claims, ensuring completeness and accuracy of information for subsequent clean claim submission, thereby minimizing delays in reimbursement.

 - Perform diligent research and follow-up on the status of primary and secondary billing claims for assigned insurance plans, proactively resolving any outstanding issues to expedite payment.

 - Review, assess, and process all claim appeals, meticulously resubmitting to insurance carriers with comprehensive and accurate supporting documentation to maximize reimbursement.

 - Evaluate customer accounts and recommend adjustments or write-offs to the Manager based on the collectability of accounts with insurance carriers or patients, maintaining a balanced approach to revenue recovery.

 - Identify and promptly report any billing problems, errors, or discrepancies to management, facilitating proactive resolution of billing trends and ensuring ongoing revenue integrity.

 - Generate and distribute monthly patient balance due statements in accordance with the explanation of benefits received from insurance carriers, fostering transparent communication, and facilitating timely resolution of outstanding balances.
   
   

Minimum Requirements:

 - One to three (3) years of experience in medical insurance claims billing and collections preferred.

 - Proficient in Google for Business, MS Office, Excel and Word.

 - Experience with physical therapy EMR systems is a plus.

 - A customer success-oriented attitude.

 - Excellent communication and negotiation skills.

 - Problem-solving aptitude.

Perks - What you can expect:

 - Competitive salaries

 - Remote/hybrid environment

 - Potential equity compensation for outstanding performance

 - Flexible PTO

 - Company-wide sponsored lunches

 - Company paid disability and life insurance benefits

 - Company paid family and medical leave

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance benefits

 - Discounted pet insurance

 - FSA/DCA and commuter ben

Job Title: QA Analyst

About Us:
At Prompt, we are leading the charge in transforming healthcare with innovative B2B enterprise software solutions tailored for rehab therapy businesses. Our mission is to improve patient outcomes and streamline workflows for rehab therapy teams by harnessing the power of advanced technology.

As part of our Quality Assurance team, you’ll help ensure that our products meet the highest quality standards and deliver meaningful value to our customers. We are building a team of ambitious, high-performing individuals who take ownership, move quickly, and think critically.

Position Overview:

As a QA Analyst at Prompt, you will be instrumental in ensuring the quality, consistency, and reliability of our software solutions. You will work closely with cross-functional teams—including product management, development, and design—to ensure our software meets the highest standards before it reaches our users.

This role will focus primarily on manual testing efforts to verify that our software performs as expected and aligns with our company’s goals. You will also play an important role in helping Prompt evolve its QA practices by testing AI-enabled functionality and leveraging modern AI and agentic tools to improve testing efficiency, coverage, and insight.

We are specifically looking for an ambitious self-starter who would be considered a high performer in any organization. The ideal candidate is highly detail-oriented, intellectually curious, proactive, and motivated by the opportunity to solve difficult problems in a fast-paced environment.

Your work will directly impact the effectiveness of our solutions for rehab therapy teams, improving their ability to deliver exceptional care to patients. This role combines deep technical expertise in AI with strong software engineering fundamentals to build reliable, production-grade systems. You’ll help us apply the latest generative AI and ML methods to real-world workflows where speed, accuracy, and results matter.

This is an opportunity to be at the forefront of real-world AI applications in healthcare—building systems used every day by clinics, staff, and patients.

Qualifications

 - 4+ years of experience building AI/ML systems in industry or academia, with meaningful production ownership

 - Strong experience building and evaluating NLP and/or LLM systems (e.g., retrieval, summarization, classification, extraction, agent workflows)

 - Track record of benchmarking and iteratively improving accuracy, cost, and latency

 - Enthusiastic and pragmatic problem-solving mindset

 - Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain complex AI concepts to non-technical stakeholders

 - Python (>3.9)

This position sits at the intersection of strategy, operations, data analysis, and execution. The ideal candidate is someone who thrives in ambiguous, fast-moving environments, enjoys structuring complex problems, and is excited to work closely with senior leadership on projects that shape Prompt’s growth, go-to-market strategy, and operational priorities.

Responsibilities

Strategic Project Management & Execution

 - Support High-Priority Strategic Initiatives: Partner closely with the Vice President of Operations to scope, coordinate, and execute strategic projects across the business. Help ensure priorities are well-defined, workstreams stay on track, and stakeholders remain aligned.

 - Cross-Functional Project Management: Support the VP of Operations in managing cross-functional initiatives involving teams such as Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, Product, Finance, and Operations. Facilitate communication, track progress, identify blockers, and drive follow-through.

 - Ad Hoc Strategic Projects: Jump into ambiguous or time-sensitive projects as needed, quickly developing context, organizing workstreams, and helping move initiatives from idea to execution.

Go-to-Market Strategy & Business Analysis

 - Pricing & Packaging Support: Assist with strategic work related to Prompt’s go-to-market motion, including pricing, packaging, segmentation, competitive positioning, and related business analysis.

 - Market Research: Conduct research on industry trends, customer segments, competitors, market dynamics, and new opportunities. Translate findings into clear, actionable summaries for leadership.

 - TAM & Market Opportunity Analysis: Support total addressable market analysis and related strategic assessments to help evaluate growth opportunities, new market verticals, and potential expansion areas.

 - New Market Vertical Exploration: Help assess potential new markets or customer segments by researching market size, customer needs, competitive landscape, operational complexity, and strategic fit.

Data Analysis & Strategic Insight

 - Data-Driven Decision Support: Use data to support strategic recommendations, business cases, and operational decisions. Build analyses that help leadership evaluate opportunities, tradeoffs, and risks.

 - Reporting & Synthesis: Gather inputs from internal systems, stakeholders, and external sources, then synthesize information into concise updates, memos, presentations, and recommendations.

 - AI-Enabled Research & Analysis: Use AI tools effectively to accelerate research, structure analysis, summarize information, and improve the quality and speed of strategic work.

Operational Structure & Problem Solving

 - Ambiguity Management: Bring structure to undefined problems by clarifying objectives, identifying key questions, organizing next steps, and driving work forward with limited direction.

 - Operational Cadence & Accountability: Help create lightweight systems for tracking projects, decisions, owners, timelines, and follow-ups across strategic initiatives.

 - Resourceful Execution: Approach new problems with curiosity, judgment, and initiative. Be willing to move fluidly between high-level strategic thinking and detailed execution.

Minimum Requirements

 - Professional Experience: 3–5 years of experience in operations, strategy, consulting
